description: The figure displays a ball-and-stick crystal structure model of the Fe-Mn-Al ternary alloy system in the B2 ordered cubic structure (CsCl-type, space group Pm-3m). The unit cell framework is delineated by purple spheres located at corners and edge centers, with green and blue-gray spheres occupying internal body-center and face-center-like positions, representing the three atomic species (Fe, Mn, and Al). This structural visualization corresponds to the first-principles DFT computational models used for the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl alloy series (x = 0 to 1), where Fe and Mn atoms share one sublattice site while Al occupies the other sublattice site. The model illustrates the atomic coordination environment and lattice configuration employed in calculations of formation enthalpy, electronic density of states, elastic constants, and Debye temperature, providing the structural basis for explaining the thermodynamic stability, mechanical anisotropy, and bonding characteristics reported in the study.
